publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 105-108. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest Mate. I was so pleased with ou sweet letter this morning, having expected none from .-- I shall be anxious to hear the results of Mr Dymond's survey. If you resided there, you might no doubt make a fine thing of it.-- Last night I saw Kayes, who I believe means to write to you himself on Wednesday.-- I made particular enquiries about the irrigation. Until Friday morning it went on , but

the sharp frost then lessened the quantity of water very much, as also yesterday morning. However it did go on a little, & this morning I do not think there has been any frost. It has been a most delicious day,-- a fine breeze, & sparkling sunshine, infusing life & brightness into everything.

I spoke to Kayes about the colt, which you were mistaken in supposing had been brought here handled. It is at Blackmore Heath, & nothing whatever has been yet done with it.-- It is 3 years old, & the coachman tells me no time must be lost, or it will be too old to break

properly, & the more as it is supposed to be promising for a lady, & likely to suit Annabella, or perhaps myself if it is tall enough to please me.-- Both Kayes & coachman agree in saying that a professional breaker must be employed. It is a very dark brown, & they say a most beautiful animal, with a head & neck that are lovely.--

I also consulted Kayes about a plan of mine for having one of our cows up when we go to Town to remain. He highly opposes it, & says she would do very well in the stables. We should have to engage, some one to come daily for an hour
or two morning & evening to see after her & the milk & cream, since I & Hester have fully considered that none of our servants could be spared.-- But this could be easily arranged.--

I have been writing today about the leather sofa cover, though not positively ordering it till I get Minter's answer; It will be very expensive, but Hester says the wear is eternal, if we have morocco, & certainly her suggestion that the canvas would be shockingly incongruous amidst all the silks & gilding & rose-wood, is very true.
She tells me that two arm-chairs which have been in constant use in Dover St for 17 years, are still quite good, being covered with red moroccco.--

Another thing I have been writing about, to Miles & , is a border to the Alhambra carpet. I find you have ordered it not to go close round the walls, but if that is the case it must have a border, for they look very bad indeed without & quite shabby as if one had not had stuff enough.-- And have you

yet given any order about the floors being polished & painted in my rooms, with a view to the empty space to be left round the carpet?-- If not, it should be done immediately, for they cannot put down the carpet till it is.--

I must ask you to re=consider about the hot closets in the laundry, which I find you desired not to be put. I assure you it is quite necessary, & that without it one cannot have our clothes dried, since they must not be hung out of doors in
London.-- So long ago as when they were building the house, you may remember my mentioning this as a sine qua [diacritical mark above the a] non.

I looked at many colours & stuffs for the curtains of my rooms, & have nearly decided on a bright light green, & the silky stuff they call .-- There was a most beautiful satin with embroidered coloured roses & flowers all over, on which I cast my eyes thinking it very suitable to the room.-- But it was unluckily 2..0..0 a yard!-- I have made

several small arrangements about rugs for the doors, & various little things, of which I am sure you will highly approve when you see them, & I assure you that I have taken durability as well as elegance into account in all my decisions. My zebra wood very much diminished on seeing more of it, & enquiring more.--
I do not send you the Sunday papers, for fear of your losing them, as I now put them by. Hester sent some letters & papers to Ashley, before I came down, by mistake.
